deck.notes: Primary browser deck exported at artifacts/browser_deck.pptx; matched MCP deck also produced at artifacts/agent_deck.pptx.

session.notes: Primary browser session exported at artifacts/browser_session.velocity; matched MCP session also produced at artifacts/agent_session.velocity.

scores.notes: Both surfaces produced the same controlled five-slide analysis set on sleep.sav without surfacing a statistics or export correctness problem.
The agent path completed cleanly, but it still depends on the explicit build, commit, and session-export sequence that the browser operator does not manage separately.
Browser and MCP outputs were materially comparable, but the exported sessions still preserve different top-level working-state details.
Both paths exported reviewable PPTX decks and portable session artifacts with the same slide titles and section structure.
The shared export stack and slide defaults were strong enough for this bounded task, though they still do not erase the browser's last-mile editability advantage.
The brief kept the task tight enough that the remaining gap was attributable to product-surface differences rather than prompt drift.

notableFindings: Both surfaces exported the same five slide titles and one shared section, Sleep Quality by Demographics.
The browser and MCP runs both produced portable session artifacts with the same saved slides and section structure.
No new blocker fix was required to execute the browser-vs-agent comparison on the intended product paths.
The main remaining difference is that browser export preserved the active problem x marital working view at the top level while the MCP session exported an empty global tableConfig.
blockers: The browser and MCP session exports are not yet identical at the global working-state layer even when the saved slides match.
The browser still has a lighter-weight last-mile editing loop than the MCP deck and session sequence.
followUps: Align top-level session export semantics between browser and MCP so active working state is preserved consistently.
Decide whether last-mile slide edit operations should become a more explicit shared engine or session primitive.
Freeze this run as the current narrow convergence baseline and continue the execution stream with EVAL-05.
